title: First War of Independence
description: Also known as the Sepoy Mutiny, it was the first widespread but unsuccessful rebellion against the British East India Company.

title: Indian National Congress Founded
description: Formed to seek a greater share in government for educated Indians, later becoming the principal leader of the Indian independence movement.

title: Swadeshi Movement
description: Started as a protest against the partition of Bengal, promoting the use of Indian-made goods over British goods.

title: Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
description: British troops fired on a large crowd of unarmed Indians in an open space known as the Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar.

title: Non-Cooperation Movement
description: Launched by Mahatma Gandhi with the aim of self-governance and obtaining full independence as the Indian National Congress.

title: Dandi March
description: The Salt Satyagraha was a major nonviolent protest action led by Mahatma Gandhi to produce salt from the seawater, breaking the salt tax law.

title: Quit India Movement
description: A movement launched at the Bombay session of the All-India Congress Committee by Mahatma Gandhi demanding an end to British rule.

title: India became Independent
description: On 15 August 1947, India gained its independence, ending almost 200 years of British rule.

title: Constitution came into force
description: On 26 January 1950, the Constitution of India came into effect, making India a sovereign democratic republic.
